evidenceBoundary.summary: This trace records observable implementation decisions, files changed, test contracts and residual risks for the second account-dashboard UI iteration and the preview DaaS data correction.

trigger.summary: The account dashboard displayed team and role data but the design remained too system-led. The preview observer account also showed ResearchOps Core Team when it should have shown DaaS.
observedIssues: The page title was too wordy and product-like.
Account summary only contained one row.
Single-team users saw a Current tag.
Users with no actions saw an empty actions section.
Permissions were shown as primary page content.
kevin.rapley@research-operations.com was shown in ResearchOps Core Team rather than DaaS.

roleReview.govukDesignSystem: Use task-appropriate components. Avoid heavy data presentation where a simple account summary is enough. Use details for supporting information not needed by most users.
roleReview.interactionDesign: Reduce cognitive load. Do not show multi-team affordances or empty actions to single-team ordinary users.
roleReview.contentDesign: Use the direct title Your ResearchOps account. Keep role labels separate from permission detail.
roleReview.informationArchitecture: Separate identity, team membership, actions and permission detail into distinct layers.
roleReview.serviceDesign: ResearchOps Core Team Admin is a distinct service state, but it should not force all users into an admin-style page.

changes: Changed H1 to Your ResearchOps account.
Removed one-row Account summary heading.
Kept Signed in as as an account summary row below the lead text.
Made account actions hidden by default.
Moved permissions into a hidden GOV.UK details component.
Cache-busted account JavaScript to account-dashboard-20260513-teams-v3.
Removed dynamic greeting title logic.
Suppressed Current tag for single-team users.
Showed actions only when available.
Showed permission details only for ResearchOps Core Team Admin with permissions.
Kept permissions separate from team membership rendering.
Created or reused active DaaS team.
Activated membership for kevin.rapley@research-operations.com in DaaS.
Assigned Observer in DaaS.
Revoked Observer in ResearchOps Core Team for that preview user.
Removed ResearchOps Core Team membership if no active roles remain.
Wrote a preview data-correction audit event.
Added PREVIEW_RESEARCH_OPERATIONS_DAAS_CORRECTION environment path.
Applied the preview DaaS correction after the first Team Admin seed.
Kept the correction out of the production deploy job.
Asserted direct page title and simplified dashboard structure.
Asserted actions are hidden when unavailable.
Asserted permission details are behind a details component and admin-only.
Asserted preview-only DaaS correction is wired to preview deployment and scoped to the preview user.

residualRisks: Direct ad hoc D1 inspection was not available through the connected tools in this chat.
The preview-only correction must be verified in the UI after the preview Worker deployment completes.
The future team-switching interaction remains out of scope for this branch.
